title: Release Notes
description: Generate human-readable, dual-audience release notes for any version of the universal portfolio + blog template. Every release note is written twice — once for end-users (non-technical, plain English, focused on what changed for them) and once for developers (technical, precise, includes migration steps and breaking changes).
prerequisite: 12-project-snapshot complete for the release version. Run before tagging a release on GitHub.
instructions: Ask the user for: the new version number, the previous version number, and the release type (patch | minor | major).
Read the git log between the two version tags (or from last tag to HEAD if releasing from current state).
Read the latest docs/snapshots/ file for context.
Read all closed GitHub issues and merged PRs since the last release.
Generate release notes in BOTH formats: end-user and developer.
For major releases: include a migration guide section.
Save to CHANGELOG.md (appended at top) and as a separate docs/releases/vX.Y.Z.md file.
After generating, ask the user to review both formats before finalising.

versioning_rules.patch: Bug fixes, security patches, documentation updates. No new features. No breaking changes.
versioning_rules.minor: New features that are backwards compatible. New config options with defaults. New optional integrations.
versioning_rules.major: Breaking changes to config file shapes, removed features, changed directory structure, changed content frontmatter fields that require migration.

end_user_release_notes.audience: Non-technical users who have deployed the template
end_user_release_notes.tone: Friendly, plain English, focused on impact not implementation

sections: ✨ New Features — what can you do now that you couldn't before, explained in plain English
🐛 Bug Fixes — what was broken and is now fixed, described by symptom not by code
⚡ Performance Improvements — what feels faster or loads better
🔒 Security Updates — reassurance that the template is kept up to date (no technical detail)
📖 Documentation — new guides or improved instructions
⚠️ Action Required — only present if the user needs to do something (update a config, rename a file)
format.example_entry: ✨ **Theme toggle now remembers your preference** — When visitors switch between light and dark mode, their choice is now saved so they see the same theme when they return.

developer_release_notes.audience: Developers forking, extending, or contributing to the template
developer_release_notes.tone: Precise, technical, includes types and file references

sections: Breaking Changes — config shape changes, removed exports, renamed files, frontmatter field changes with before/after examples
New Features — new components, new lib functions, new API routes, new config options with types
Bug Fixes — issue number, root cause, fix approach
Performance — what changed, why, measured impact if available
Security — CVE references if applicable, dependency upgrades, new mitigations
Dependencies — added, removed, upgraded packages with version numbers
ADRs — new or superseded ADRs in this release
Migration Guide — step by step instructions for upgrading from previous version (major releases only)
format.example_entry: **Breaking:** `WorkItem.externalLink` renamed to `WorkItem.externalUrl` to match URL field naming convention (ADR-006). Update all `content/work/*.mdx` frontmatter files: rename `externalLink:` to `externalUrl:`.

sections: Overview — what changed and why (link to ADR)
Step-by-step migration instructions numbered precisely
Before/after code examples for every breaking change
Automated migration script if possible (codemods)
What to test after migrating
Rollback instructions

changelog_format.structure: Newest release at top. Each release has: version header, date, end-user summary (2-3 sentences), then full developer notes.
changelog_format.unreleased_section: Always maintain an [Unreleased] section at the top for changes not yet tagged

release_checklist: npm audit — no high or critical vulnerabilities
CHANGELOG.md updated with new version at top
Git tag created: git tag -a vX.Y.Z -m 'Release vX.Y.Z'
GitHub Release created with end-user release notes as the body
Live demo site redeployed and verified

release_types_guide.v0.x.x: Pre-release / template in active development. Breaking changes allowed without major version bump.
release_types_guide.v1.0.0: First stable release. Config file shapes are considered stable API. Breaking changes require major version.
release_types_guide.v1.x.0: Minor releases — new features, new optional config fields, new integrations.
release_types_guide.v1.x.x: Patch releases — bug fixes, security patches, documentation improvements.

outputs: CHANGELOG.md — updated with new version entry at top
End-user release notes (ready to paste as GitHub Release body)
guardrails: Never write release notes from memory — always read the git log and closed issues first.
Never omit breaking changes from the developer notes — even small ones.
Never describe a bug fix in technical terms in the end-user notes — describe the symptom and the resolution.
Never release a major version without a migration guide.
The [Unreleased] section in CHANGELOG.md must be cleared and moved to the new version entry on every release.
Never bump the version in package.json without updating CHANGELOG.md in the same commit.
